content.text:
{The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from the Lord.}{Proverbs 16:33}
 {The theory of determinants is the central part of algebra.}{— Arthur Cayley}

To find eigenvalues, we solve a polynomial equation derived from 
the matrix.

This has a nonzero solution iff $A - \lambda I$ is singular, i.e., 
$\det(A - \lambda I) = 0$.

content.title: Characteristic Polynomial and Equation
content.content:
The **characteristic polynomial** 
of an $n \times n$ matrix $A$ is:
\[
p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)
\]
The **characteristic equation** 
is $\det(A - \lambda I) = 0$.

content.title: Finding eigenvectors
content.problem: Find eigenvectors for the eigenvalues found above.

content.title: Algebraic and Geometric Multiplicity
content.content:
- The **algebraic multiplicity** 
 of eigenvalue $\lambda$ is its multiplicity as a root of the 
 characteristic polynomial.

- The **geometric multiplicity** 
 is $\dim(E_\lambda)$, the dimension of the eigenspace.

marginNotes.text:
In the day of prosperity be joyful, 
and in the day of adversity consider.
